goal: Implement P1.1: make the orchestrate loop's pause button actually halt execution. The pause_requested flag exists on OrchestrateRun and the UI endpoint flips it, but the executor loop never checks it — users click Pause yet the run keeps consuming tasks and budget.

tasks.name: Add _check_pause() helper and cooperative gate in orchestrate loop
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/models/orchestrate_run.py: (1) add pause_requested: Mapped[bool] mapped_column(nullable=False, server_default="false"), paused_at: Mapped[dt.datetime | None] DateTime(timezone=True), resumed_at: Mapped[dt.datetime | None] DateTime(timezone=True); (2) extend CheckConstraint to include PAUSED status; (3) add schema migration in platform/app/services/schema_migrations.py to ADD COLUMN pause_requested bool default false, paused_at timestamptz, resumed_at timestamptz if not exist. In platform/app/api/pipeline.py: (4) add _check_pause(db, run_id) helper mirroring _check_cancel; (5) in orchestrate() loop, after candidate found and before claiming (before candidate.status = IN_PROGRESS), call _check_pause and if True: _update_run status=PAUSED paused_at=now current_phase=paused, return stopped_reason=paused.

acceptance_criteria.text: _check_pause(db, run_id) returns True when pause_requested=True in DB (fresh read)
Orchestrate loop sets status=PAUSED when flag is set

produces.pause_gate: cooperative _check_pause() in orchestrate loop, model has pause_requested/paused_at/resumed_at

tasks.name: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/pause endpoint
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/pipeline.py, add @router.post("/orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/pause"). Mirror orchestrate_run_cancel: fetch run (404 if missing), 409 if status in (DONE, FAILED, CANCELLED, BUDGET_EXCEEDED, PAUSED), set run.pause_requested=True, db.commit(), return {pause_requested: True, status: run.status}. Add tests for: pause sets flag, pause on finished/paused returns 409.

tasks.name: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume endpoint
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/pipeline.py, add @router.post("/orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/resume"): fetch run (404 if missing), 409 if status != PAUSED, set run.pause_requested=False, run.status=RUNNING, run.resumed_at=now(utc), db.commit(), return {resumed: True, status: RUNNING}. Note: the background loop exited on pause — resume clears the flag for the next /orchestrate invocation. Add tests: resume on PAUSED clears flag sets RUNNING sets resumed_at; resume on non-PAUSED returns 409.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume on PAUSED run returns 200, status=RUNNING, clears pause_requested, sets resumed_at

tasks.name: Expose pause fields in run status endpoint and render Resume in UI panel
tasks.instruction: Two changes: (A) In platform/app/api/pipeline.py orchestrate_run_status() (~line 1308), add pause_requested, paused_at (isoformat or null), resumed_at (isoformat or null) to returned dict. (B) In platform/app/templates/_orchestrate_panel.html: add Pause button next to Cancel in RUNNING/PENDING block (hx-post to UI pause route); add PAUSED status badge color; add PAUSED block showing paused_at and Resume button (hx-post to UI resume route with page reload). In platform/app/api/ui.py, find existing /ui/orchestrate-runs/{id}/cancel route and add matching /pause and /resume routes that call the API and redirect back.

acceptance_criteria.text: GET /orchestrate-runs/{id} response includes pause_requested, paused_at, resumed_at fields
Pause button renders in RUNNING state and Resume button renders in PAUSED state in _orchestrate_panel.html

produces.pause_ui: Pause/Resume UI in orchestrate panel, pause fields in status API
